hero.bodyLine1: Hotel Berlin, Berlin is where travellers, locals, and ideas naturally meet.
hero.bodyLine2: Sunlight fills the courtyard, artists make real work,
hero.bodyLine3: and the hum of Berlin is never far away.
hero.ctaSrOnly: at Hotel Berlin, Berlin (opens booking page)
hero.mapsLink: Get directions to Hotel Berlin, Berlin at Lützowplatz 17, Tiergarten (opens Google Maps)
hero.satelliteAlt: Map showing Hotel Berlin, Berlin at Lützowplatz 17, Tiergarten

rooms.label: Sleep & Relax
rooms.title: Sleep & Relax
rooms.body: Whether it’s your first time in Berlin or you’re a seasoned traveller, flying solo or arriving with the family our spacious, quiet rooms and suites are your place to land. Thoughtful design details and a relaxed, home-like feel make every room a personal retreat. This is where your Berlin story begins.

meetings.label: Meet & Work
meetings.title: Serious business, playful spaces
meetings.body: Business is in our DNA. With over 4,000 m² of flexible conference and meeting spaces, cutting-edge event technology, and a dedicated team, we ensure everything from conferences to workshops runs smoothly — leaving space for ideas and connections to take the lead.
meetings.galleryAria: Hotel Berlin, Berlin meeting room photos

culture.label: Arts & Culture
culture.title: The building is the programme
culture.gridAria: Arts and culture at Hotel Berlin, Berlin

fkkb.subtitle: Contemporary art
fkkb.title: Freundeskreis der Künstler Berlin
fkkb.body: FKKB has been part of the hotel since the 1980s — a working gallery where artists make real work, not just display it. Open to guests and the public.

kttk.subtitle: Table tennis
kttk.title: KTTK — Königlicher Tischtennis Klub
kttk.body: Four JOOLA tables in the basement. Open to guests and Berliners. Thursday nights — tournament. No booking, no dress code.

sissi.subtitle: Urban culture
sissi.title: Sissi — Skateboard Museum
sissi.body: One of Europe's largest skateboard collections — decks, graphics, and Berlin skate history. A love letter to the city's counterculture.

haus-luetzowplatz.subtitle: Art centre
haus-luetzowplatz.title: Haus am Lützowplatz
haus-luetzowplatz.body: Berlin's oldest art association — exhibitions, talks, and a café on the square. Two minutes from the hotel.
haus-luetzowplatz.imageAlt: Art exhibition at Haus am Lützowplatz

lutze.name: Lütze
lutze.heading: The place to eat, play, and hang all day.
lutze.body: In the heart of the hotel — open to guests and Berliners alike. Breakfast from the counter. Lunch on the terrace. Cocktails until the city stops. Happy hour isn't a time slot. It's a state of mind.
lutze.galleryAria: Lütze restaurant and bar at Hotel Berlin, Berlin
lutze.ctaVisitAria: Visit Lütze website (opens in new tab)

status.kitchenClosing: Kitchen closing soon · bar stays open

map.label: Neighbourhood
map.title: You're in the right part of Berlin
map.subtitle: Tiergarten, the canal, Ku'damm — all on foot

neighbourhood.label: Neighbourhood
neighbourhood.title: Around the hotel
neighbourhood.subtitle: Parks, galleries, restaurants, and Berlin life — all within walking distance of Lützowplatz.
neighbourhood.mapAria: Interactive map of places near Hotel Berlin, Berlin
neighbourhood.mapUnavailable: Map is loading — add a Mapbox access token to enable the interactive map.

checkin-time.question: What are the check-in and check-out times?
checkin-time.answer: Check-in is from 15:00. Check-out is by 12:00 noon. Early check-in from 06:00 is available for €30 if your room is ready — or book the night before for guaranteed early access. Self-service kiosks are available in the lobby.

parking.question: Is there parking at the hotel?
parking.answer: Yes — underground parking with over 200 spaces. €4 per hour, maximum €25 per day. EV charging is available.

pets.question: Are pets welcome?
pets.answer: Yes. Pets are welcome — a fee of €30 per night applies. They're also welcome at breakfast.

airport.question: How do I get here from BER airport?
airport.answer: Take the RE7 or RB14 to Zoologischer Garten, then Bus 100 to Lützowplatz — right outside the hotel. Around 45–55 minutes total.

bed-types.question: What bed configurations are available?
bed-types.answer: All room categories are available with either a king bed or twin configuration. Request your preference at booking — subject to availability.

room-size.question: How large are the rooms?
room-size.answer: Superior rooms are 22–26 m². Comfort rooms are 26–32 m². Suites range from 40–65 m². Studio 45 is available on request.

cancellation.question: What is the cancellation policy?
cancellation.answer: Flexible rate bookings can be cancelled free of charge until 18:00 on the day of arrival. Non-refundable rates are charged in full at time of booking.

extra-beds.question: Can extra beds or cots be added?
extra-beds.answer: Cots are available for children under 2 at no charge. Rollaway beds can be added to most room types for €30 per night, subject to availability.

capacity.question: What is the largest event space?
capacity.answer: The Berliner Saal accommodates up to 600 guests in theatre configuration. Total event space across all rooms is over 4,000 m².

av-tech.question: What AV and technology is included?
av-tech.answer: All meeting rooms include high-speed Wi-Fi, screen or projection, and sound system. Full AV packages including live streaming and simultaneous translation are available.

catering.question: Is catering included in meeting packages?
catering.answer: Day delegate packages include coffee breaks and lunch. Bespoke catering from Lütze is available for all events.

parking-meet.question: Is parking available for day delegates?
parking-meet.answer: Underground parking is available at €4 per hour. Pre-bookable delegate parking rates are available — ask the events team when enquiring.

lutze-open.question: What are Lütze's opening hours?
lutze-open.answer: The bar is open daily from 10:00. The kitchen serves 11:30–15:00 and 17:00–22:30. Vinyl Nights are every Monday from 18:00.

reservations.question: Do I need to book a table?
reservations.answer: Reservations are recommended for dinner but not required. Walk-ins are always welcome at the bar. For large groups of 8 or more, please book in advance.

dietary.question: Are dietary requirements catered for?
dietary.answer: Yes — vegan, vegetarian, and gluten-free options are available. Please mention any allergies or dietary requirements when booking.

kttk-book.question: Do I need to book a table tennis table?
kttk-book.answer: No booking needed — tables are available on a first-come basis during open hours. Thursday tournament nights are open to all. €5 entry.

footer.bookingStrip: Best rate guaranteed when you book direct
footer.transit: Bus 100, 106, 187 · U Nollendorfplatz 7 min · S+U Zoo 10 min
footer.alreadyHereDesc: Your guest hub — events, dining, neighbourhood picks, and everything you need during your stay.
